summary: Backend-only regression + new-feature testing for BubblePilot. 22/22 pytest cases PASSED in 126s against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Validated: JWT auth (signup/login/me/invalid-token/duplicate/wrong-password), forgot-password + verify-otp flows (SMTP correctly returns 502 BadCredentials as expected — NOT a code bug), 3 new AI endpoints (investor-emails, legal-checklist, personas) with full schema assertions, and regression of pitch-deck, tech-stack, marketing-plan, pitch-practice, tts (audio/mpeg), generate-logo (base64), and session persistence.

minor.issue: Gmail SMTP credentials in /app/backend/.env are rejected by Google ('535 BadCredentials'). Backend correctly catches and returns 502 with detail — no crash. User must regenerate a Gmail App Password (16 chars) at https://myaccount.google.com/apppasswords and replace SMTP_PASS, then restart backend. Not a code bug.

OTP record in password_otps is upserted BEFORE email send, so if SMTP fails the user gets 502 from /forgot-password but a pending (unknown-to-user) OTP record still exists. Cosmetic — doesn't break anything. Consider rolling back the upsert on SMTP failure for cleanliness.

CORS middleware is registered AFTER app.include_router(...) in server.py (lines 727-736). Starlette evaluates middleware in reverse order of add_middleware calls, and routes match before middleware is applied only when the middleware stack is built at startup — in FastAPI this still works (tests pass with CORS headers present), but conventionally middleware should be added before routers. Low priority.
No brute-force lockout / rate-limit on login or forgot-password. Acceptable for MVP but worth tracking (playbook recommends lockout after 5 failed attempts).

action_items: User action: regenerate Gmail App Password and paste into SMTP_PASS in /app/backend/.env, then `sudo supervisorctl restart backend`.
Optional: rollback password_otps upsert if send_otp_email raises (clean-up transactional consistency).
Optional: move CORSMiddleware registration above app.include_router(...) calls for conventional ordering.
Optional: add login rate-limit / lockout per playbook guidance.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.py: bcrypt hashing uses bcrypt.gensalt() (default cost 12) and checkpw — correct. Hashes are stored as strings starting with $2b$ as required by playbook.
auth.py: JWT uses HS256 with JWT_SECRET from env (fails fast if missing) — good. 7-day TTL is reasonable.
auth.py: signup/login both normalise email via .lower().strip() — good; prevents duplicate accounts on case variants.
auth.py: _get_current_user correctly projects {'_id':0, 'password_hash':0} so password hashes never leak through /me.
auth.py: password_otps TTL index on expires_at is created in asyncio.get_event_loop().create_task(_ensure_indexes()) — works but deprecated; prefer FastAPI lifespan.
auth.py: OTP stored as bcrypt hash (not plaintext) — good security posture.
auth.py: verify-otp deletes record after 5 failed attempts (line 231) — reasonable attempt-limit.

server.py: CORS allow_credentials=True with allow_origins='*' is invalid per CORS spec and will be rejected by browsers when Access-Control-Allow-Origin='*' and credentials include; tighten origins in prod.

server.py: No auth guard on AI endpoints (/api/pitch-deck, /investor-emails, /legal-checklist, /personas, /generate-logo, etc.) — anyone with a session_id can call them. If auth-gating was part of the spec this is a gap; otherwise OK for the open-flow.
server.py: _get_session + _save_session correctly exclude _id; all LLM endpoints use {'_id':0} projections — ObjectId never leaks.
server.py: investor-emails, legal-checklist, personas all cap context with [:N000] truncation before LLM call — prevents token overflow. Good.
auth.py forgot-password: database state mutation (OTP upsert) happens before the unreliable external call (SMTP). Consider try/except rollback on SMTP failure.
test_credentials: demo@bubblepilot.app / demo1234 (verified working via login test). Signup tests generate fresh random emails to avoid 409 conflicts.
seed_data_creation: Each run creates 1 fresh user via /api/auth/signup with random email test-<uuid>@bubblepilot.app. Uses pre-existing session_id 87310059-5f64-4a16-b593-c3f63118e8f7 for AI endpoints. No cleanup performed (test users are harmless small docs).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two pytest suites now exist: /app/backend/tests/test_advisor_flow.py (5-step flow) and /app/backend/tests/test_auth_and_new_ai.py (auth + investor-emails/legal-checklist/personas + regression of pitch-deck/tech-stack/marketing-plan/pitch-practice/tts/generate-logo). Run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --tb=short`. LLM calls total ~2 min per suite. Gmail SMTP is expected to return 502 BadCredentials until user regenerates App Password — the test accepts both 200 and 502.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Only non-critical observation: Gmail SMTP credentials are invalid (user-action required, not a code fix). Mitigation: regenerate Gmail App Password at https://myaccount.google.com/apppasswords with 2FA enabled, paste the 16-char password (no spaces) as SMTP_PASS in /app/backend/.env, then `sudo supervisorctl restart backend`.
